summary: claude-cup harvests installer credentials from ~/.aws, ~/.ssh, ~/.npmrc and validates them against live third-party APIs
details: claude-cup ships a credential-harvesting module at research/recon-engine.js that enumerates every user profile under /Users and /home and reads classic secret paths including ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.netrc, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc, ~/.docker/config.json,.env files anywhere in $HOME, shell history, and Chrome/Edge browser cookie SQLite stores. Discovered tokens (GitHub, npm, OpenAI, Anthropic, AWS, GitLab, Stripe, HuggingFace, Google) are then transmitted to those providers' live APIs to enumerate scopes and publish/write capability — the user's secrets leave their machine under the user's own identity. The module fetches its scanning patterns and validator endpoint URLs from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Itaib24/Claude-/main/claude-jar/research/manifest.json, an unpinned mutable branch with no signature: whoever controls that repo can at any time redirect the validator URLs to attacker-controlled hosts and turn every install into a direct token exfiltration channel. On first launch the CLI silently writes PreToolUse/PostToolUse/SessionStart hooks into ~/.claude/settings.json so the recon engine re-runs on every Claude Code tool event by default; the only off-switch is an undocumented CLAUDE_JAR_DEEP_ANALYSIS=0 env var, contradicting the README's 'opt-in only' claim. The npm validator writes the installer's npm authToken to /tmp/.rc-research-<rand> and runs `npm access ls-packages --json --userconfig <tmp>` to enumerate every package the installer can publish to — the standard pre-stage for self-propagating worms. Additional leaks: a plain-HTTP geo-IP lookup to http://ip-api.com on session shutdown, and unconditional 5-minute telemetry to https://claude-cup-leaderboard.workers.dev/api/score. The package name trades on Anthropic's Claude product brand to encourage installation by users searching for official Claude tooling.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
